title: Gloxinia × youngeana
taxonomy: Gloxinia × youngeana is a hybrid of Gloxinia and Sinningia species. It belongs to the family Gesneriaceae. The classification includes it being a part of the genus Gloxinia, but now often placed in Sinningia. This change in classification reflects its hybrid origin and similarity to Sinningia species. It is native to Brazil and is often cultivated for its beautiful and long-lasting flowers.
uses: Gloxinia × youngeana is known for its various medicinal uses, primarily in traditional medicine. The plant is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. However, further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of these medicinal uses.

intro: Gloxinia × youngeana, commonly known as Younghusband's Gloxinia, is a rare and endangered hybrid plant species within the Gloxinia genus, characterized by its unique, bell-shaped flowers and dark green, heart-shaped leaves. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Gloxinia × youngeana is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. Bioactive constituents of this plant include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Gloxinia × youngeana is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is used to treat various ailments, including fever, colds, and respiratory issues.
